message:
feat(watchdog): Cx-first health check for MuxWatchdog (ft-xbnl0.2.2)

Add MuxWatchdog::check_cx so the pane-list ping and watchdog-warnings
probe both bind their timeout boundaries to an explicit &Cx. Extracts
the shared sample-finalization logic into finalize_sample to avoid
duplicating it across the Cx/non-Cx/fallback paths.

New Cx-first API (asupersync-runtime only):
- MuxWatchdog::check_cx(&mut self, cx: &Cx) -> MuxHealthSample

Implementation:
- check() builds a Cx via Cx::current().unwrap_or_else(for_request)
  under asupersync-runtime and delegates to check_cx (matching the
  established pattern). Non-asupersync fallback uses check_inner.
- check_cx uses timeout_with_cx for both the ping and warning probes.
- finalize_sample (new, non-async) computes status, manages the
  history ring buffer, and returns the sample.

New deterministic LabRuntime test:
- mux_watchdog_check_cx_runs_under_labruntime (seed
  0xD0C8_D06C_C410_00AF): creates a mock_wezterm_handle, calls
  check_cx, and asserts ping_ok + Healthy status in <2s wall-clock.

Note: ipc.rs has a pre-existing test compilation error (IpcAuthToken
field rename by a concurrent agent); this prevents running the full
`watchdog::` test filter but does not affect watchdog code correctness.
The LabRuntime test passes when run individually.

Sixth file in the ft-xbnl0.2.2 Cx-first lane. Remaining: 9 files.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
